description: Side-scrolling locomotion: horizontal run, gravity, jump, and coyote-time. Sets velocity for a following velocity integrator. Treats entity.body.contacts.onGround (set by the collision-resolution phase when a collider rests on a solid) as grounded, so a tile floor or solid body satisfies the jump test. The genre-feel layer is OPTIONAL params, each defaulting to the original behavior: run acceleration/friction (accel/friction), variable jump height (jumpCutMultiplier), jump buffering (jumpBuffer), apex hang (apexGravityMult/apexThreshold), and drop-through (down + jump on a one-way platform falls through it, via down/dropThroughTime). 1.3.0 adds LADDER climb: with climbSpeed set, when the entity's center is over a ladderProp tile and up/down is held it climbs (gravity off, vy = ±climbSpeed) and can step off the side.

jumpSpeed.description: Upward jump launch velocity in px/sec.

maxFall.description: Terminal fall speed in px/sec (0 = uncapped).

accel.description: Horizontal acceleration in px/sec² toward the target speed (0 = instant vx).

friction.description: Horizontal deceleration in px/sec² when there is no input (0 = use accel; only applies when accel > 0).

jumpCutMultiplier.description: vy is multiplied by this on jump-release while rising (1 = no cut / fixed-impulse jump).

jumpBuffer.description: Seconds a jump press is remembered so it fires on landing (0 = off).

apexGravityMult.description: Gravity multiplier while |vy| < apexThreshold (1 = no change).

apexThreshold.description: |vy| in px/sec under which apexGravityMult applies (0 = off).

down.description: Drop-through modifier key codes (empty = drop-through off).

dropThroughTime.description: Seconds the drop-through window stays open after down+jump on a one-way platform (0 = off).

ladderProp.description: Tile-property flag marking a climbable ladder cell (default "ladder").

climbSpeed.description: Ladder climb speed in px/sec (0 = ladders off).

up.description: Ladder climb-up key codes (default ArrowUp/W; on a ladder these climb, not jump).
